title: EVA games
wikitext:
[]: EVA games             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Dec 30, 2008 8:18 pm What are your fave EVA games? I would love to play Super Robot Wars (though not TECHINCALLY an EVA game), Shinji Ikari Raising Project, Girlfriend of Steel and 2nd Impression. What are your favorites? Post me and tell oyur top pics and some info on them.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 30, 2008 9:07 pm So far I've played (or beaten): GoS1 fansub, GoS2, Eva N64 (only got past Sachiel after that Shinji kept dying ), Evangelion 2 for PS2, Evangelion 2 for PSP, Ayanami Raising Project with Asuka Completion Project (PS2). Currently playing: Shinji Ikari Raising Project (at least when I've found the time). I want to play First and Second Impression (the former was released as a DVD game a few years back) because I'm a completest like that and the clips I've seen look interesting. My favorite Eva game so far: Evangelion 2. The PS2 version is as good as the PSP version, but the PSP version has a lot more features. I strongly recommend picking up this game not just because Anno had a hand in it not just with the CI files and overall, but also because it really is the best adaption of an Eva game so far.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Tue Dec 30, 2008 9:14 pm I need a PS2, and to know where I can find copies of NGE2, not 2 mention a sega saturn and copies of the other games. ;(

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Thu Jan 01, 2009 12:39 pm Well, I'd say that the top of my waiting list right now for games with Eva characters in them would be SRW Alpha for the PSX. Gideon Zhi just "finished" and released a translation patch of SRW Alpha Gaiden, and he commented that the two games were similar in their engines, as well as Tyria already having finished a literal translation of all the game's text. I'd say within the next 6 months, we'll be able to get our hands on this Evangelion (plus other) RPG.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Jan 02, 2009 2:20 am I tried to play the demo of the Ayanami Raising Project, though I didn't have the slightest clue what I was doing most of the time.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Jan 03, 2009 9:04 pm Count A'ight wrote: I tried to play the demo of the Ayanami Raising Project, though I didn't have the slightest clue what I was doing most of the time.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 03, 2009 11:12 pm I'm partial to So Cubed's Evangelion Dating Sim RPG.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 04, 2009 2:50 pm Ah yes, that one is pretty cool. heh

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Sun Jan 04, 2009 5:24 pm Oh yeah! Season of Sakura is one of the best H-games I've ever played, and it's based more off of the alternate universe from episode 26 then anything else. Sticks you in the shoes of "not Shinji", and lets you befriend "not Kensuke", have a mandatory sex lesson from "not Misato", and the choice of dating "definitely not Asuka or Rei". Plus, the story is pretty damn good. Not that I make a habit of playing H-games or anything, mind you. <_<

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Sun Jan 04, 2009 5:32 pm sure, tbk........ sure........ we believe you right, guys? ;) *shhh*

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Jan 04, 2009 6:48 pm TheBastardKing: I googled info about that and loled. Anyway, yeah. Back on topic about Eva games, though Eva2 is definitely the best one I've played, I think GoS1 and GoS2 have their charm in that cheesy sappy dating sim way.

[]:             Quote Post by Magami no D00M » Sun Jan 04, 2009 7:15 pm I've only ever played the aforementioned "Dating Sim" from So Cubed ego, but other than that, just battle orchestra. And i never got to Gunbuster...*sniff* Haven't had the time for the PS2 as of late anyway, so coming close to completing this simple Smash Bros clone (feels like one in many respects) will have to wait.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Jan 15, 2009 3:43 pm I've only ever played was the one for the N64 and it was pretty good other then the battle with Ramiel which was pretty hard for me to beat.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jan 15, 2009 3:52 pm I would REALLY like to play Eva2. It's kind of weird - the whole layout of the game reminds me a bit of persona. Don't you think Eva could work well in a persona format? Damn, I need to get a ps2 one of these days...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 15, 2009 4:12 pm Image of Shinji shooting himself in the head to summon eva 01... and failing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 15, 2009 4:12 pm I thought I mentioned it in this thread (was probably somewhere else then), but what's nice about the PSP version of Eva2 is PSP consoles themselves are Region-free for games (UMD videos, however, are encoded only for the region your PSP is from. ) If you get the PS2 version, however, you'd need to look into a converter to play import games (If you Google "Swap Magic" you should find something.) Hope that helps. Anyway, the Eva2 game itself is a combination of a world simulator/raising game (eg, taking care of character needs like eating or watching TV), dating sim and RPG rolled into one. I really think it's the best adaption of Eva into a game, personally.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Thu Jan 15, 2009 6:26 pm It does sound like the ultimate fanfic creator's dream. It's more the fact that it's only in Japanese that's stopping me getting it than anything else... ...there's an online petition for this already, isn't there?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 15, 2009 6:29 pm Even better, there's this gem written by somebody who happens to be a forum member: http://www.gamefaqs.com/portable/psp/file/930008/45735

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Thu Jan 15, 2009 6:59 pm I've played GoS2 and Evangelion 64. GoS2 is just stupid. EVA 64, on the other hand, is actually pretty interesting. The fighting gameplay sucks, but some of the battles are actually minigames that replicate what exactly what happened in the TV series . And considering the console, the graphics are simply marvelous.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jan 15, 2009 7:07 pm maya's goal is to date aoba? um...
